description: Nearest package.json type determines whether .js is ESM or CommonJS in Node.
body: Use when diagnosing Node import/export behavior. Do not apply to files already forced by .mjs or .cjs or to code bundled before Node sees it. The agent should inspect nearest package.json before changing syntax. Verify with the Node error disappearing under the same script. Risk boundary is monorepos where nested packages have different type fields. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
